message:
fix(rpc): host2netname uses libc::malloc to match caller libc::free (bd-dqqh1)

## Root cause (bisection)

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test under default
parallelism reproduced free(): invalid size SIGABRT locally for the
first time (bd-dqqh1, opened earlier this turn). Bisection by
test-name pattern at --test-threads=4:

    --test rpc_abi_test 'host2netname_' --test-threads=2 -> SIGABRT
    --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_creates_valid_name N=4 -> 1/1 pass
    --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_null_defaults     N=4 -> 1/1 pass

Either test alone passes; the two together at N>=2 abort. They share
NO test-side state — but BOTH allocate via host2netname() and BOTH
free via libc::free().

The bug at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs:2874:

    let buf = unsafe { crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc(name.len()) } as *mut c_char;

host2netname allocates through OUR internal arena (raw_alloc), but
the test releases via libc::free (host glibc). In debug builds (test
context, no LD_PRELOAD), libc::free binds at link time to glibc's
free — which sees a chunk header from our arena, interprets it as a
glibc chunk-size field, gets nonsense, and aborts. Race vs serial is
just chunk-layout entropy: with one thread the arena's per-thread
chunk metadata happens to look benign; with 2+ threads it diverges
deterministically.

## Fix

Switch host2netname to libc::malloc. POSIX defines host2netname as
"returns a buffer the caller must free()", so the alloc/free pair
must match what the caller's free() resolves to:

- Under LD_PRELOAD: libc::malloc IS our interposed malloc; pairs with
  caller's libc::free which IS our interposed free. Same allocator
  end to end.
- Under non-preload (test/debug binaries): libc::malloc is glibc's;
  pairs with caller's libc::free which is glibc's. Same allocator end
  to end.

raw_alloc was wrong for both contexts: under preload it's our internal
arena (different from our public malloc), under non-preload it's an
unrelated arena from glibc's view. Either way, the free() can't match.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test (default parallelism)
  -> 35 passed, 0 failed (was SIGABRT free(): invalid size).

All thread counts 1..8 now pass.

## Wider implication — bd-zgifl filed

A grep for `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc` across the abi crate finds
30+ similar call sites returning buffers to callers in
inet_abi.rs, dirent_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, more in rpc_abi.rs,
wchar_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, and string_abi.rs. Each is a candidate
sibling for the same cross-allocator-free abort. Filed bd-zgifl as
the umbrella bead with the per-callsite list and recommended sweep
(replace raw_alloc with libc::malloc, add regression test under
cargo-test default parallelism).

The bd-ye0f4 cluster (a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 partial fixes
chasing TLS-dtor isolation) was almost certainly chasing different
manifestations of the SAME root cause — different test paths, same
cross-allocator-free shape. With this bisection in hand, every other
recurrence has a concrete fix template.

Closes bd-dqqh1.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -637,7 +637,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-di5w","title":"Track A: Implement _IO_iter_* native stream iteration over NativeStreamRegistry","description":"## Goal
Replace 4 stub _IO_iter_* functions in io_internal_abi.rs (lines 1430-1457) with real implementations that iterate over the NativeStreamRegistry.

## Current State
All 4 functions return null, making the stream list appear empty:
- _IO_iter_begin() -> null (should return first occupied slot index)
- _IO_iter_end() -> null (should return sentinel past last slot)
- _IO_iter_file(iter) -> null (should return FILE* for the slot)
- _IO_iter_next(iter) -> null (should advance to next occupied slot)

## Why This Matters
glibc atexit/fflush-all path may walk _IO_list via these iterators. If the list is empty, exit-time flushing of native streams is silently skipped by any code using glibc's _IO_iter API. Our native fflush(NULL) works via NativeStreamRegistry.flush_all(), but external code (atexit handlers in host glibc) that walks _IO_list via iterators would miss our streams.

## Design
Use slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding:
- _IO_iter_begin: Lock registry, find first OCCUPIED slot (index 0..255), return (index+1) cast to *mut c_void. Return null if no occupied slots. We use index+1 so that 0 (stdin at slot 0) is distinguishable from null.
- _IO_iter_end: Return (STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY+1) cast to *mut c_void as sentinel. This is 257, always > any valid encoded slot.
- _IO_iter_file: Decode (iter as usize - 1) to get slot index, call registry.get(index), return &NativeFile as *mut c_void. Return null if index is invalid or slot is empty.
- _IO_iter_next: Decode current slot index from iter, scan forward for next OCCUPIED slot, return encoded index or end sentinel.

### Thread Safety
The registry is behind a Mutex. Each iterator call acquires and releases the lock independently. This means the iteration is NOT atomic — streams could be added/removed between calls. This matches glibc behavior (glibc's _IO_iter functions are also not atomic without _IO_list_all_lock held externally).

### Implementation Helpers
Add to NativeStreamRegistry:
- first_occupied() -> Option<usize>: scan slots[0..] for first SLOT_OCCUPIED
- next_occupied(after: usize) -> Option<usize>: scan slots[after+1..] for next SLOT_OCCUPIED

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs: Replace 4 function bodies, add 2 helpers to NativeStreamRegistry

## Unit Tests (in tests/io_internal_native_file_test.rs)
- test iter_begin_returns_first_stream: Register 0 extra streams -> begin iterates over stdin/stdout/stderr (3 entries)
- test iter_begin_end_empty_after_unregister: Unregister all stdio, begin == end (no streams)
- test iter_file_returns_valid_native_file: iter_file on valid iterator returns non-null with correct fd
- test iter_file_on_end_returns_null: iter_file on end sentinel returns null
- test iter_next_advances_to_next_occupied: skip empty slots correctly
- test iter_next_at_end_returns_end: advancing past last stream returns end sentinel
- test iter_full_traversal_count: register 5 streams, iterate begin->next->next...->end, count matches open_count()
- test iter_survives_concurrent_unregister: unregister a stream during iteration, no crash

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_io_iter_native.sh
Build and run a focused Rust test binary that:
1. Opens 10 files via our native fopen (if available) or memfd_create
2. Walks _IO_iter_begin -> _IO_iter_next until _IO_iter_end
3. Counts entries and verifies >= 10
4. Emits structured JSONL trace per step

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=io_internal, symbol=_IO_iter_begin|end|file|next, slot_index, result_ptr, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 _IO_iter_* functions return real iterators over NativeStreamRegistry
- 8+ unit tests pass covering positive, negative, edge, and concurrency cases
- e2e script passes
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) not regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:17.216902967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935547570Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935252511Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_iter_begin/end/file/next over NativeStreamRegistry using slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding. Added first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers. 8 new integration tests: begin returns first stream, end is constant sentinel, file returns valid NativeFile, file on end/null returns null, next past end stays at end, full traversal visits all streams, traversal after unregister skips freed slots. All 40 io_internal tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io-internal","native","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1361,"issue_id":"bd-di5w","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implementation note: NativeStreamRegistry already has get() and open_count(). Use slot index as opaque pointer (usize cast to *mut c_void). Sentinel for _IO_iter_end = STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY (256). Lock registry mutex during scan. Consider exposing first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers on NativeStreamRegistry.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dq9t","title":"setjmp: execute setjmp_nested_edges fixture programs through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/setjmp_nested_edges_conformance_test.rs only validates tests/conformance/fixtures/setjmp_nested_edges.json metadata. Add executable coverage for the documented program scenarios through the harness matrix or its deterministic integration path, assert strict and hardened expectations for nested longjmp and sigmask round-trip behavior, and preserve explicit unsupported-scenario documentation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:06:43.306090252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:42.520337201Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:42.518294051Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic execution coverage for setjmp nested-edge fixture programs across strict and hardened profiles.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dqbv","title":"nss: execute grp_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/grp_ops_conformance_test.rs currently validates fixture metadata and coverage counts only. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/grp_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity by mode, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:43:33.470596927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:48:23.475094946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:48:23.472922473Z","close_reason":"Added isolated harness-matrix execution for grp_ops.json and verified locally and via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-dqqh1","title":"[HIGH] rpc_abi_test parallel race: free(): invalid size SIGABRT at --test-threads>=4 (locally reproducible bd-ye0f4 sibling)","description":"## Symptom

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test under default cargo
parallelism aborts with free(): invalid size:

    running 35 tests
    RPC: Success
    RPC: Timed out
    RPC: Unknown error
    free(): invalid size
    error: test failed, to rerun pass -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test
    process didn't exit successfully: .../rpc_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)

The 3 RPC lines come from clnt_perrno_does_not_crash, which runs
early. Abort fires AFTER those lines, meaning another parallel-
running test is the culprit, not clnt_perrno itself.

## Bisection (LOCAL — unlike bd-ye0f4 which only reproduced on rch)

    --test-threads=1: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=2: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=4: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size
    --test-threads=8: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size (default)

Locally reproducible on a 64-core x86_64 host at HEAD 17431c7a —
threshold is exactly at --test-threads=4. This is the first
locally-reproducible sample of the bd-ye0f4 cluster's
'free(): invalid size' signature. Previous cluster beads (bd-g2bd5,
bd-ye0f4) only reproduced under rch remote execution with the
identical glibc error.

## Why this matters

The bd-ye0f4 cluster has been cycling fix→regress since
2026-04-22 across a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 (all partial TLS
dtor scoping fixes). Every round left the race live. Root cause
has been elusive because nobody could reproduce it locally — only
on rch workers with specific worker scheduling. This rpc_abi_test
sample is a deterministic local reproducer: same
'free(): invalid size' glibc detection, pinpoints the race at the
4+ thread boundary, and runs every time.

## Diagnosis candidates

clnt_perrno_does_not_crash routes through crate::unistd_abi::write.
write() fans into runtime_policy::decide (complex state machine
with potentially-racy internal globals) and runtime_policy::observe
(recording validations). Other tests in the same binary exercise
rpc_thread_createerr, rpc_thread_svc_*, rpc_thread_tls_* — thread-
local getters that allocate buffers.

The most likely explanation: one test's thread-local RPC state is
freed by another thread's teardown (thread-local cross-allocator
free), or runtime_policy has a per-thread AtomicI32 / RefCell that
gets torn-down by one test while another test is mid-decide.

## Fix direction

1. Re-run with RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=3 (already tried, no
   extra info — glibc's native free() abort is silent). Next level:
   compile with RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' (nightly) and rerun
   the 4-thread test to get an ASan stack trace pointing at the
   freeing site and the allocating site.
2. Audit all thread-locals in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
   and crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs for allocation
   Drop impls that could free pointers allocated by a different
   thread's allocator. rpc_abi.rs has SPERRNO_BUF and
   SPCREATEERR_BUF (Cell<[u8; N]>) which are inline arrays — safe.
   But __rpc_thread_createerr, __rpc_thread_svc_fdset,
   __rpc_thread_svc_max_pollfd, __rpc_thread_svc_pollfd return
   pointers into allocations — those are the likely culprits.
3. Check the 3+ rpc_thread_* tests: if they allocate in one test
   thread and the Drop fires on the next test thread (pthread-local
   rebinding), we get cross-allocator free.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=2
  -> 35 passed, 0 failed.
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=4
  -> SIGABRT, free(): invalid size.

No commit changes needed to repro. Provides the long-awaited local
sample for the bd-ye0f4 cluster.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:29:08.914661512Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:29:08.914661512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","free-invalid-size","full-review","locally-reproducible","race","rpc","test"]}
+{"id":"bd-dqqh1","title":"[HIGH] rpc_abi_test parallel race: free(): invalid size SIGABRT at --test-threads>=4 (locally reproducible bd-ye0f4 sibling)","description":"## Symptom

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test under default cargo
parallelism aborts with free(): invalid size:

    running 35 tests
    RPC: Success
    RPC: Timed out
    RPC: Unknown error
    free(): invalid size
    error: test failed, to rerun pass -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test
    process didn't exit successfully: .../rpc_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)

The 3 RPC lines come from clnt_perrno_does_not_crash, which runs
early. Abort fires AFTER those lines, meaning another parallel-
running test is the culprit, not clnt_perrno itself.

## Bisection (LOCAL — unlike bd-ye0f4 which only reproduced on rch)

    --test-threads=1: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=2: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=4: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size
    --test-threads=8: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size (default)

Locally reproducible on a 64-core x86_64 host at HEAD 17431c7a —
threshold is exactly at --test-threads=4. This is the first
locally-reproducible sample of the bd-ye0f4 cluster's
'free(): invalid size' signature. Previous cluster beads (bd-g2bd5,
bd-ye0f4) only reproduced under rch remote execution with the
identical glibc error.

## Why this matters

The bd-ye0f4 cluster has been cycling fix→regress since
2026-04-22 across a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 (all partial TLS
dtor scoping fixes). Every round left the race live. Root cause
has been elusive because nobody could reproduce it locally — only
on rch workers with specific worker scheduling. This rpc_abi_test
sample is a deterministic local reproducer: same
'free(): invalid size' glibc detection, pinpoints the race at the
4+ thread boundary, and runs every time.

## Diagnosis candidates

clnt_perrno_does_not_crash routes through crate::unistd_abi::write.
write() fans into runtime_policy::decide (complex state machine
with potentially-racy internal globals) and runtime_policy::observe
(recording validations). Other tests in the same binary exercise
rpc_thread_createerr, rpc_thread_svc_*, rpc_thread_tls_* — thread-
local getters that allocate buffers.

The most likely explanation: one test's thread-local RPC state is
freed by another thread's teardown (thread-local cross-allocator
free), or runtime_policy has a per-thread AtomicI32 / RefCell that
gets torn-down by one test while another test is mid-decide.

## Fix direction

1. Re-run with RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=3 (already tried, no
   extra info — glibc's native free() abort is silent). Next level:
   compile with RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' (nightly) and rerun
   the 4-thread test to get an ASan stack trace pointing at the
   freeing site and the allocating site.
2. Audit all thread-locals in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
   and crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs for allocation
   Drop impls that could free pointers allocated by a different
   thread's allocator. rpc_abi.rs has SPERRNO_BUF and
   SPCREATEERR_BUF (Cell<[u8; N]>) which are inline arrays — safe.
   But __rpc_thread_createerr, __rpc_thread_svc_fdset,
   __rpc_thread_svc_max_pollfd, __rpc_thread_svc_pollfd return
   pointers into allocations — those are the likely culprits.
3. Check the 3+ rpc_thread_* tests: if they allocate in one test
   thread and the Drop fires on the next test thread (pthread-local
   rebinding), we get cross-allocator free.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=2
  -> 35 passed, 0 failed.
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=4
  -> SIGABRT, free(): invalid size.

No commit changes needed to repro. Provides the long-awaited local
sample for the bd-ye0f4 cluster.","notes":"

## ROOT-CAUSE FIX by cc-libc (2026-04-22)

Bisected by running rpc_abi_test with progressively narrower test-name filters at --test-threads=4. Convergence:

    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test 'host2netname_' -- --test-threads=2  -> SIGABRT free(): invalid size
    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_creates_valid_name -- --test-threads=4  -> 1/1 pass
    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_null_defaults     -- --test-threads=4  -> 1/1 pass

Either test alone passes; the two together at N>=2 abort. They share no test-side state — but they BOTH allocate via host2netname() and BOTH free via libc::free().

The actual bug at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs:2874:

    let buf = unsafe { crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc(name.len()) } as *mut c_char;

host2netname allocates through OUR internal arena (raw_alloc), but the test releases the buffer via libc::free (host glibc's free). In debug builds (test context, no LD_PRELOAD), libc::free binds at link time to glibc's free — which sees a chunk header from our arena, interprets it as a glibc chunk-size field, gets nonsense, and aborts with 'free(): invalid size'.

The race is just chunk-layout entropy: with 1 thread the same allocator context is reused so chunks happen to look fine; with 2+ threads the arena's per-thread chunk metadata diverges from glibc's expectations and the free hits a bad size word deterministically.

## Fix

Change host2netname to allocate via libc::malloc instead of our internal raw_alloc. POSIX defines host2netname as 'returns a buffer the caller must free()' — that means the caller's free() (glibc's in non-preload context, our interposed one in preload context) must match the alloc. libc::malloc satisfies both: in preload context it IS our interposed malloc, in non-preload context it's glibc's, and either way it pairs correctly with libc::free.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test (default parallelism) -> 35 passed, 0 failed (was SIGABRT). All thread counts 1..8 now pass.

## Wider implication for the bd-ye0f4 cluster

30+ other call sites use the same `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc` pattern and return the buffer to the caller (inet_abi.rs, dirent_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, more in rpc_abi.rs, wchar_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, string_abi.rs). Each is a candidate sibling failure. Every test that exercises one of those entrypoints AND frees with libc::free is a potential next bd-dqqh1. Filed as an umbrella bead for systematic audit.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:29:08.914661512Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:56:51.292886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:56:51.292886Z","close_reason":"Fixed: host2netname now uses libc::malloc instead of internal raw_alloc; alloc/free pair consistent with caller libc::free in both LD_PRELOAD and non-preload contexts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","free-invalid-size","full-review","locally-reproducible","race","rpc","test"]}
 {"id":"bd-drs1c","title":"fuzz: printf/snprintf format-string adversarial invariants (width/precision/specifier chaos)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_printf_adversarial.rs covering format-string **security** properties beyond what fuzz_printf already does. fuzz_printf focuses on correctness of well-formed specifiers with structured args; this target covers **attacker-controlled format strings**: huge widths/precisions, positional-arg reordering, %n refusal, bracket-bomb '%*.*' chains, and buffer-exhaustion safety.

## Archetype
1 crash-detector + invariant (security contract):
- snprintf with huge width (1M) must not OOM or overrun dst buffer
- %n must either be honored with a valid ptr OR return -1 / refuse — never crash or write to garbage
- positional '%99$d' with missing args must return an error rather than reading OOB stack

## Inputs
struct PrintfAdversarialFuzzInput {
  format: Vec<u8>,           // arbitrary bytes — fuzzer can generate '%' + junk
  int_args: [i32; 8],        // padded arg vector
  str_args: [Option<Vec<u8>>; 4],
  dst_size: u16,
}

## Invariants
- Return value <= dst_size - 1 (or -1)
- Written bytes fit in dst_size (guard-byte sentinel check)
- Determinism on repeated calls with identical args
- No panic on ANY format byte sequence (including partial '%' at NUL, unknown specifier, repeated length modifiers)

## Expected bug classes (per CVE history)
- glibc CVE-2023-25139: %n within width-modifier chains
- glibc CVE-2010-0296: formatted-printing stack exhaustion for deeply nested positional refs
- Integer overflow in computed width (we widened i32 width to c_int during bd-0u5fm but the WIDTH field inside pattern '%2147483648d' is still parsed — test with decimal-string widths that overflow usize)
- Off-by-one in our own buffer-truncation path

## Relation to existing work
bd-m40be (fnmatch), bd-7rxtm (strftime), bd-luc3d (length-modifier ABI) already found format-parser bugs via fuzzing. This target fills the final gap: the printf pipeline itself, beyond the structured-fixture-case harness.

## Score
5 × 4 × /2 ≈ 10 — CVE-class severity (format-string attacks remain a top-10 web-server vector).","notes":"Closed by cc-libc.

Delivered crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_printf_adversarial.rs with 8 archetypes covering the CVE classes listed in the bead:

- adv_parser_panic: arbitrary-byte inputs to parse_format_string / parse_format_spec (termination + bounded-segment invariant).
- adv_huge_dynamic_width: %*d with attacker-chosen width int.
- adv_bracket_bomb: %*.*d chaining width and precision magnitudes.
- adv_bracket_bomb_string: %*.*s variant with attacker-supplied string.
- adv_percent_n_refusal: %n under hardened mode — must be honored (count==2) or refused (rc<0 or count==-1), never write to garbage.
- adv_overflow_width_literal: integer-overflow widths encoded as compile-time literals (%2147483648d, %99999999999d, %.99999999999d, %9999999999.9999999999d) so we never pass attacker-controlled formats to a C variadic call.
- adv_positional_out_of_range: %99$d with only one arg — must not read past supplied varargs.
- adv_percent_storm: pure-% format string — parser stress.

All archetypes use 64-byte GUARD_BYTE sentinels on both sides of the destination buffer; each call asserts the guards are untouched after return. Hardened mode is set once globally via sync::Once.

Safety: the target never passes an attacker-controlled format to a variadic C call (that would be UB at the language level). Attacker chaos lands in the numeric args, in the Rust parser entrypoints, or in a fixed set of compile-time format literals.

Wired into crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml as `fuzz_printf_adversarial` bin.

Validation: cargo check --bin fuzz_printf_adversarial clean (13s on vmi1149989); cargo clippy --bin fuzz_printf_adversarial --no-deps -- -D warnings clean (local fallback because remote worker lacks nightly-2026-04-13).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:40:18.636316852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:17:52.575665374Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:17:52.573399840Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-drs1c","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:46.383999511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-dss9","title":"Conformance: execute poll_ops fixture cases","description":"Extend the poll_ops conformance test so every poll/select fixture case runs through frankenlibc_fixture_exec::execute_fixture_case and compares against expected_output.  {"id":"bd-dvr22","title":"[HIGH] fuzz gap: signal/socket/fortify/setjmp/mmap/dlfcn/pthread/c11threads ABI modules are unfuzzed (mmap now partially covered by fuzz_mmap)","description":"## Motivation

/testing-fuzzing skill discovery pass on crates/frankenlibc-abi/src enumerated 3646 `pub unsafe extern "C" fn` symbols across 38 modules and cross-referenced against the 30 fuzz bins registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml. The CVE-class gap is concentrated in 8 modules that currently have **no** fuzz target:

| Priority | Module | Symbols | CVE class |
|---------|--------|---------|----------|
| 1 | mmap | 11 | memory-safety (UAF / OOB / W^X bypass) — **covered by fuzz_mmap in same PR** |
| 2 | signal | 29 | handler setup races, async-signal-safety, privilege escalation |
| 3 | socket | 18 | network attack surface, kernel ABI complexity |
| 4 | fortify | 79 | `__*_chk` overflow-detection wrappers — if broken, they silently fail to protect |
| 5 | setjmp | 12 | control-flow integrity, sigsetjmp edge cases |
| 6 | dlfcn | 7 | dlopen/dlsym attack surface |
| 7 | pthread | 141 | concurrency (mutex/cond/rwlock/barrier) — best done as TSan campaign |
| 8 | c11threads | 21 | C11 threading (thrd_/mtx_/cnd_) — same pattern as pthread |

## Status of priority-1 (mmap)

Delivered in the same push: `crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_mmap.rs` — stateful harness with a live-mapping arena, 7 ops (Map/Unmap/Protect/Sync/Advise/Remap/MarkStaleInPlace), guard-byte sentinels on writable mappings, alignment invariant on mmap/mremap return addresses, and the use-after-unmap contract (every op on a Stale slot must return -1 / MAP_FAILED). The harness intentionally exercises PROT_EXEC / RWX requests against the hardened-mode membrane to cover the expected refuse-or-clamp path. Registered as `fuzz_mmap` bin. Validation: cargo build + cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings both clean.

## Fix direction for the remaining 7

Each should follow the pattern already established by the 5 fuzz targets cc-libc shipped earlier this session (fuzz_printf_adversarial, fuzz_pathname, fuzz_env, fuzz_sched, fuzz_pidfd_timer):

- Stateful archetype with a handle table where applicable (signal → signal handler slots; socket → sockfds; pthread → thread / mutex / cond handles; setjmp → jmp_buf storage slots).
- Return-code contract + lifecycle invariant (stale handle → must fail).
- Direct syscall differential where possible (signal, socket via libc::syscall). For pthread/c11threads, use a BTreeMap / counter shadow model because the ABI deviates from raw syscall shape.
- Safety bounds: signal restricted to {0, SIGWINCH, SIGURG} the way fuzz_pidfd_timer does; socket in PF_LOCAL with SOCK_DGRAM + AT_EMPTY_PATH style so no real network traffic.
- TSan-specific campaign for pthread/c11threads; run with `RUSTFLAGS=-Zsanitizer=thread`.

## Score priority

Per /testing-fuzzing skill, score = (untrusted-input) * (complexity) * (unsafe/native) * (prior CVEs). The mmap surface scores highest because of 40+ years of mmap/munmap/mprotect CVEs (CVE-2017-1000405 ptrace race, CVE-2016-5195 'Dirty COW' mprotect interaction, CVE-2023-40283 vma-use-after-free). Signal is second (CVE-2020-14386 af_packet, CVE-2021-44733 tee), fortify third (CVE-2023-4911 'Looney Tunables' via __stack_chk_* interaction).

## Exit criteria

One child bead per remaining module, each:
- Delivers a fuzz target in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/
- Registers it in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml
- Achieves ≥1000 exec/s (skill Hard Rule 1) or, for stateful harnesses, ≥100 exec/s
- Cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings clean
- Seed corpus committed (exclude artifacts per existing .gitignore)

+{"id":"bd-zgifl","title":"[HIGH] cross-allocator-free pattern: 30+ raw_alloc sites return buffers callers libc::free (bd-dqqh1 cluster)","description":"## Pattern audit (bd-dqqh1 generalization)

Following the bd-dqqh1 root cause (host2netname allocates via internal arena, caller frees via libc::free → free(): invalid size SIGABRT), grep for the pattern `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc` across crates/frankenlibc-abi/src returns 30+ call sites. Each one returns a heap buffer to its caller. If the caller is C user code under LD_PRELOAD, our interposed free() catches it correctly. If the caller is a Rust test binary (debug build, no preload), libc::free binds to glibc's free at link time and the cross-allocator free aborts with the same fingerprint.

## Confirmed sites (free-by-caller pattern, POSIX 'caller must free')

inet_abi.rs:382, 393   - getifaddrs / gethostbyname pattern
dirent_abi.rs:524, 556, 567 - scandir family
stdlib_abi.rs:4026     - realpath et al
rpc_abi.rs:666, 667, 1091, 1151, 1228, 1293 - XDR/RPC strdup-likes
rpc_abi.rs:2995        - host2netdomain (sibling of host2netname, same shape)
wchar_abi.rs:76, 1315, 4365 - wcsdup et al
unistd_abi.rs:3335, 3544, 4907, 4927, 6985, 7002, 7026, 7046,
              7218, 7224, 9868, 10097, 16246
                       - getcwd / wordexp / getifaddrs / strdup-likes
string_abi.rs:3484     - strdup probably

Each site allocates with raw_alloc (our internal arena) and the caller is expected to free via the standard free(). This works under LD_PRELOAD (our interposed free routes correctly) but fails on debug-test builds where libc::free binds to glibc's free.

## Bead bd-dqqh1 fix shape

`let buf = libc::malloc(name.len()) as *mut c_char;` instead of `raw_alloc(name.len())`. Under LD_PRELOAD libc::malloc IS our interposed malloc, so functionality is preserved. Under non-preload (test context) libc::malloc is glibc's, which pairs correctly with libc::free.

## Sweep recommendation

For each call site listed above:

1. Confirm the buffer is returned to a CALLER (vs freed in same module).
2. Confirm the contract is 'caller must free()' (POSIX docs / glibc man pages).
3. Replace raw_alloc with libc::malloc.
4. Add a regression test that calls the function under cargo test default parallelism, frees with libc::free.

Rough scope: ~30 mechanical edits + ~30 regression tests. Could be split per-module: rpc (5 callsites), unistd (13), wchar (3), inet (2), dirent (3), stdlib (1), string (1).

## Provenance

bd-dqqh1's host2netname fix at HEAD post-17431c7a fixed one site of the pattern. This umbrella bead tracks the remaining 30+. The bd-ye0f4 cluster (a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 partial fixes) was likely chasing different facets of the SAME root cause.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:57:08.590918911Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:57:08.590918911Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","allocator","cross-allocator","full-review","heap","malloc","umbrella"]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y","title":"EPIC: Eliminate all remaining host delegations (resolve_host_symbol_raw to 0)","description":"## Goal
Eliminate every remaining resolve_host_symbol_raw call across the ABI crate (except host_resolve.rs which IS the resolver infrastructure), bringing FrankenLibC to zero host libc runtime dependencies for its classified symbol surface.

## Background
As of commit d974b43, 64 host delegations were eliminated (57 from unistd_abi.rs, 6 from rpc_abi.rs, 1 from glibc_internal_abi.rs). Code audit reveals 31 remaining calls across 7 files (excluding host_resolve.rs):

### Remaining by file (31 total, verified by code audit)
| File | Calls | Unique Symbols | Category |
|------|-------|---------------|----------|
| io_internal_abi.rs | 7 | 3 (_IO_list_all, _IO_file_jumps, _IO_wfile_jumps) | glibc stream internals |
| setjmp_abi.rs | 6 | 4 (setjmp, _setjmp, __sigsetjmp, longjmp/siglongjmp) | register save/restore |
| stdio_abi.rs | 5 | 5 (stdin/stdout/stderr, setvbuf, host fn cache) | host FILE* bootstrap |
| pthread_abi.rs | 5 | 5 (getattr_np, cond_clockwait, timedjoin, tryjoin, clockjoin) | pthread extensions |
| dlfcn_abi.rs | 4 | 3 (dlopen x2, dlsym, dlclose) | dynamic loader |
| stdlib_abi.rs | 3 | 3 (setenv, unsetenv, putenv) | environ array |
| resolv_abi.rs | 2 | 2 (getaddrinfo, freeaddrinfo) | DNS resolution |

Note: host_resolve.rs has 3 calls but is the resolver infrastructure itself — not a promotion target.

### Two categories of remaining delegations
**Category A — Intentional Interpose Delegations (18 calls):**
These delegate to host ONLY for objects the host created (non-managed). Native paths already work for FrankenLibC-managed objects. Correct for LD_PRELOAD mode; dead code in replace mode.
- pthread_abi.rs: 5 (all conditional on !is_managed_*)
- dlfcn_abi.rs: 3 (dlsym/dlclose for host-opened handles)
- stdio_abi.rs: 2 (setvbuf + host fn cache for non-registry FILE*)
- stdlib_abi.rs: 3 (prefer host to avoid environ ownership conflict)

**Category B — Genuinely Missing Native Implementations (13 calls):**
These need new code — the host delegation is the primary/only path.
- setjmp_abi.rs: 6 (UNCONDITIONAL — always delegates, needs assembly)
- io_internal_abi.rs: 7 (bootstrap host glibc _IO_* structures)
- stdio_abi.rs: 3 (stdin/stdout/stderr global resolution)
- resolv_abi.rs: 1 (getaddrinfo DNS fallback — /etc/hosts already native)
- dlfcn_abi.rs: 1 (dlopen bootstrap passthrough)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero resolve_host_symbol_raw calls in ABI crate except host_resolve.rs
- All existing tests pass (156 lib + 113 unistd + 88 stdio + 35 rpc + 21 dlfcn + 21 setjmp + 163 pthread + all others)
- Full workspace clippy clean
- Each sub-bead includes: unit tests, e2e script, structured logging, verification commands
- Category A beads may be deferred to replace-mode milestone if interpose behavior is correct","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:41:10.103151134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:09.722735542Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:09.722366513Z","close_reason":"ALL 7 children closed: bd-zh1y.1 (pthread extensions), bd-zh1y.2 (setjmp/longjmp asm), bd-zh1y.3 (native FILE/vtable/registry), bd-zh1y.4 (stdio globals), bd-zh1y.5 (dlfcn classification), bd-zh1y.6 (environ ownership), bd-zh1y.7 (getaddrinfo DNS). Host delegations reduced from ~102 to ~8 (5 intentional dlfcn interpose + 3 host_resolve infrastructure). LD_PRELOAD smoke: 58/0/6.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","host-delegation","native"],"comments":[{"id":1344,"issue_id":"bd-zh1y","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"Created by ChartreuseCrane after eliminating 64 host delegations (57 unistd, 6 rpc, 1 glibc_internal). This epic tracks the remaining 35 (38 minus 3 in host_resolve.rs which must stay). Subtasks ordered by difficulty: pthread (MEDIUM, most independent), setjmp (HARD, assembly), io_internal (HARD, architecture), stdio (MEDIUM, blocked on io_internal), dlfcn (HARD, loader), stdlib (MEDIUM, startup), resolv (HARD, DNS). Recommended execution: pthread first, then stdlib, then setjmp, then io_internal+stdio together, then resolv, then dlfcn last.","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:15Z"},{"id":1346,"issue_id":"bd-zh1y","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"KEY INSIGHT (ChartreuseCrane): After deep code analysis, the remaining host delegations fall into TWO categories: (1) INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE DELEGATIONS — delegate to host for objects the host created (pthread condvars/threads/mutexes, dlopen handles, stdin/stdout FILE*). These are CORRECT for LD_PRELOAD mode and would be dead code in replace mode. Includes: 5 pthread, 5 dlfcn, 5 stdio, 3 stdlib. (2) GENUINELY MISSING native implementations — require new code to eliminate. Includes: 7 setjmp (assembly), 8 io_internal (native FILE struct), 2 resolv (DNS). The epic should track both, but priority should be on category (2) since category (1) is correct interpose behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:48:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.1","title":"Promote pthread extension APIs to native futex/syscall (5 delegations)","description":"## Goal
Classify 5 pthread host delegations as intentional interpose behavior and ensure native paths are robust.

## Classification: INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE DELEGATIONS
All 5 delegations are CONDITIONAL — they only fire for non-managed (glibc-created) objects. For FrankenLibC-managed objects, native paths already exist and work via futex/syscall. This is correct LD_PRELOAD behavior.

### Current code structure (verified by audit)
1. pthread_getattr_np (line 3658): delegates for non-managed threads; native path reads /proc/self/task/[tid] for managed threads
2. pthread_cond_clockwait (line 5185): delegates for non-managed condvars; native path uses futex with clock conversion for managed condvars
3. pthread_timedjoin_np (line 5237): delegates for non-managed threads; native path uses futex-based timed wait for managed threads
4. pthread_tryjoin_np (line 5332): delegates for non-managed threads; native path checks managed thread state atomically
5. pthread_clockjoin_np (line 5401): delegates for non-managed threads; native path converts clock and delegates to timedjoin

### What needs to happen
Phase 1 (this bead): Verify native paths are complete and correct. Add comprehensive tests. Add FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING/FORCE_NATIVE_MUTEX e2e coverage. Document the intentional delegation pattern.
Phase 2 (replace-mode milestone): When FrankenLibC owns all thread creation, non-managed paths become dead code. Remove host delegation at that point.

## Implementation tasks
1. Add unit tests for each extension API on managed objects (native path exercised)
2. Add e2e test script that sets FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING=1 and exercises all 5 APIs without host delegation
3. Add a guard assertion: if FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING is set, resolve_host_symbol_raw must never be reached (add debug_assert or metric)
4. Update support_matrix classification for these 5 symbols to Implemented (native path) with interpose note

## Testing requirements
- Unit tests: nominal + edge cases for each API (timeout=0, timeout=past, invalid args, EINVAL paths)
- e2e script: scripts/check_pthread_extensions_native.sh exercising all 5 with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING=1
- Structured log fields: trace_id, api_family=pthread, symbol, mode (native vs host), decision_path, errno, latency_ns
- Verify: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test (all 163 tests pass)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.015769400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T21:04:43.801009502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T21:04:43.800725133Z","close_reason":"All 4 child beads closed: bd-zh1y.1.1 (getattr_np /proc maps), bd-zh1y.1.2 (cond_clockwait futex), bd-zh1y.1.3 (tryjoin/timedjoin native), bd-zh1y.1.4 (clockjoin native). All 5 pthread extension APIs have dual native+host paths with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING test coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["host-delegation","native","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.1","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:44:33.015769400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1345,"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.1","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"ANALYSIS UPDATE (ChartreuseCrane): After reading the code, all 5 pthread host delegations are INTENTIONAL INTERPOSE DELEGATIONS, not missing implementations. They only fire for non-managed (glibc-created) objects. For FrankenLibC-managed objects, native paths already exist and work. In replace mode (future), these would be dead code since all objects would be managed. For interpose mode, delegation for non-managed objects is CORRECT. Subtasks should be reclassified: .1.1 getattr_np is the only one that could benefit from improvement (eliminating the populate_managed_attr_from_host_attr translation step). The others (.1.2-.1.4) are already correctly implemented for managed objects.","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:47:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.1.1","title":"Native pthread_getattr_np via /proc/self/task maps parsing","description":"Implement pthread_getattr_np by reading /proc/self/task/[tid]/maps to find stack boundaries, combining with thread-local attribute state (detach state, guard size, stack size). This is what glibc does internally. Must handle main thread (stack at top of address space) vs spawned threads (stack allocated by pthread_create).","acceptance_criteria":"pthread_getattr_np returns correct stack base/size and detach state for managed threads. Unit tests: spawn thread, call getattr_np, verify stack size >= PTHREAD_STACK_MIN, verify detach state matches creation args. e2e: scripts/check_pthread_extensions_native.sh with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING=1.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:46:09.218021356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:44:50.146623867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:44:50.146249178Z","close_reason":"Implemented stack_bounds_from_proc_maps() fallback in pthread_getattr_np. Parses /proc/self/task/<tid>/maps for [stack] annotation (main thread) and SP containment (spawned threads). Added 2 tests: main thread and std::thread via /proc maps with FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING. All 6 getattr_np tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:46:09.218021356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
